name: Zero-Point Energy Research
what: Exploration of zero-point energy and its potential applications.

name: Simulation Theory and Multiverse Engineering
what: Speculative theories on the simulation hypothesis and engineering multiverses.

name: Consciousness Theories and AI
what: Emerging theories on artificial consciousness and their implications for AI development.

name: Civilisation-Scale Engineering Projects
what: Concepts and plans for Dyson spheres, orbital rings, and stellar lifting technologies.

name: Quantum Internet Protocols
what: Development of protocols for secure quantum communication networks.

name: Quantum Gravity Hypotheses
what: Exploration of quantum gravity theories and their implications for physics.

name: Quantum Sensing Innovations
what: Development of quantum sensors for precision measurements in various fields.

name: Quantum Computing Breakthroughs
what: Advancements in fault-tolerant quantum computing and quantum internet infrastructure.

name: BCIs Enabling AI Interfaces
what: Brain-computer interfaces that facilitate direct communication between the brain and AI systems, improving human-AI interaction.

name: Neural Data for AI Training
what: Using neural data from brain recordings to train and improve AI systems, enhancing their performance in various applications.

name: CNT Tethers
what: Carbon nanotube-based tethers for space elevators and other large-scale structures.

name: Aerogel Insulation
what: Lightweight, high-insulating materials used in various applications from space suits to building insulation.

name: Radiation-Resistant Materials
what: Materials designed to withstand high levels of radiation, crucial for space exploration and nuclear applications.

name: mRNA Platforms
what: Advanced mRNA technologies for delivering therapeutic proteins and vaccines, with applications in gene therapy and beyond.

name: CAR-T Cell Engineering
what: Engineering T cells to target and destroy cancer cells, a key therapy in immunotherapy.

name: Gene Therapy
what: Therapeutic techniques that use modified genes to treat or prevent diseases, often delivered via viral vectors.

name: Genetic Circuit Design AI
what: AI-driven methods to design complex genetic circuits for synthetic biology applications.

name: Protein Design AI (AlphaFold2)
what: AI tools that design proteins with specific functions, enabling new applications in biotechnology and medicine.

name: Battery Chemistry AI
what: AI-driven research and development of new battery chemistries for improved energy storage solutions.

name: Fusion Plasma Control AI
what: AI technologies that enhance the control and stability of fusion plasma in tokamaks for more efficient energy production.

name: Grid Optimisation AI
what: AI systems that optimize the distribution and management of electrical power in smart grids.

name: Materials Project
what: A database of materials properties and predictions that uses machine learning to accelerate the discovery of new materials.

name: Generative Materials Design
what: AI-driven methods to design new materials with specific properties for various applications.

name: AI Drug Discovery
what: Using machine learning to predict the efficacy of new drugs based on molecular structure and properties.

name: AlphaFold
what: A pioneering protein structure prediction tool that has revolutionized drug discovery and genetic research.

name: Dyson Swarm Milestone Roadmap
what: Outlines the steps required to build a network of solar panels in space to capture vast amounts of energy.

name: Space-Based Solar Power Satellites
what: Deploys solar power satellites in orbit to transmit energy back to Earth via microwaves or lasers.

name: Lunar Base Construction
what: Plans the construction of a permanent lunar base for scientific research and resource extraction.

name: Programmable Matter
what: Develops materials that can change shape or properties based on external stimuli, enabling new applications in robotics and manufacturing.

name: Topological Insulators for Electronics
what: Explores the use of topological insulators in next-generation electronics for improved performance and reduced energy loss.

name: MAX Phase Ceramics
what: Utilizes MAX phase ceramics in high-temperature applications due to their excellent thermal stability and mechanical strength.
source: Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory

name: Green Ammonia Production
what: Develops sustainable methods for producing ammonia using renewable hydrogen and nitrogen sources.

name: Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS)
what: Expands geothermal energy production by drilling deeper into the Earth’s crust to access hotter temperatures.

name: OTEC (Ocean Thermal Energy Conversion)
what: Harnesses the temperature difference between surface water and deep ocean water to generate electricity.

name: mRNA Therapeutics Beyond Vaccines
what: Explores the use of mRNA technology for treating various diseases beyond vaccines, including genetic disorders and autoimmune conditions.

name: CAR-T 2.0 Therapies for Solid Tumors
what: Enhances chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapies to target solid tumors more effectively.

name: Liquid Biopsy for Early Cancer Detection
what: Uses circulating tumor cells (CTCs) to detect early-stage cancer through a simple blood test.
source: Altos Labs, Bryan Johnson’s Precision Oncology

name: Autonomous Drone Logistics Networks
what: Establishes a network of autonomous drones for efficient delivery services, reducing carbon footprint and improving supply chain resilience.

name: Precision Fermentation at City Scale
what: Utilizes advanced biotechnology for large-scale production of proteins and other bio-products in urban environments.

name: On-device AI for Smart Cities
what: Develops on-device AI to process sensor data in real-time, reducing latency and increasing security.

name: Large-Scale 3D-Printed Structures
what: Construction techniques using large-scale 3D printing to build complex structures.

name: Autonomous Bricklaying & Rebar Robots
what: Automated robots designed to lay bricks and place rebar in construction projects.

name: Fully Adaptive Governance Systems
what: AI-driven governance systems that adapt to changing conditions and optimize decision-making processes.

name: National Digital Twins
what: Digital representations of physical infrastructure and systems used for predictive maintenance, optimization, and emergency response.

name: Cognitive Prosthetics (Memory & Attention)
what: Advanced neural prosthetics designed to enhance cognitive functions such as memory and attention.

name: CAR-T 2.0: Next-Generation T Cell Therapies
what: Next-generation CAR-T therapies aim to improve the specificity, durability, and safety of cancer immunotherapies by using advanced genetic engineering techniques.

name: Stellar Engineering
what: Stellar engineering involves manipulating stars to alter their properties, such as size or luminosity. This speculative technology could have profound implications for space exploration and resource management.
source: Breakthrough Starshot · Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics

name: Post-Biological Civilisation
what: A post-biological civilisation refers to a future where biological life is no longer the dominant form of intelligence. This concept explores how advanced AI and other technologies might lead to new forms of societal organization and existence.
source: Future of Life Institute · Oxford Martin School
